g.title: Expresses (refers to): Fill in the vpc_id, project_id and normalized_cluster_name in the "cluster.yaml" file, and adjust the values to your liking. The defaults should be sufficient for a POC cluster of Openshift. → cluster.yaml

Expresses (refers to): Fill in the vpc_id, project_id and normalized_cluster_name in the "cluster.yaml" file, and adjust the values to your liking. The defaults should be sufficient for a POC cluster of Openshift. → cluster.yaml

Expresses (refers to): Repeat the above process to create RHCOS image for control plane nodes and worker nodes respectively, so that they boot and fetch ignition configs from the master.ign and worker.ign files in your s3 bucket. This will be the image ids you confgure for control plane and worker nodes in the "cluster.yaml" file later → cluster.yaml

Expresses (refers to): Repeat the above process to create RHCOS image for control plane nodes and worker nodes respectively, so that they boot and fetch ignition configs from the master.ign and worker.ign files in your s3 bucket. This will be the image ids you confgure for control plane and worker nodes in the "cluster.yaml" file later → cluster.yaml
Expresses (involves): $ shell command openshift-install wait-for install-complete --dirinstlller_dir → instlller
Expresses (involves): $ shell command openshift-install wait-for install-complete --dirinstlller_dir → instlller
run command and wait for result
Note this can be made more granular and also included in the Terraform config

Once there exist RHCOS images that reads the "user_data" provided by the 0xide metadata service (NoCloud) creating tailored images with kernel params for fetching ignition configs will not be necessary anymore. · The setup has been tested with "Openshift enterprise 4.20", however it should work equally well with OKD, as long as the install config is adjusted accordingly.
This is some notes regarding semi automated setup of openshift 4.20 on the 0xide cloud computer. · I consider it work in progress, and the notes are intended for my own remembering.

g.text: so that they boot and fetch
This will be the image ids

g.title: run command and wait for result · This approves the worker nodes into the cluster
run command and wait for result
